steps.title: Match season to fitness
steps.content: The assertion says fruit flies collected in cooler months should tend to have lower reproductive fitness. Because wing centroid size is used as a proxy for body size, and body size correlates with fitness, the evidence should compare wing size between a warmer month and a cooler month.

steps.title: Reject weak data uses
steps.content: Choice A uses male values that do not provide the needed accurate table support. Choice B compares male and female wing sizes within months rather than showing a seasonal change. Choice C gives only temperature, so it omits the wing-size proxy for reproductive fitness.

steps.title: Choose the seasonal comparison
steps.content: The useful comparison keeps the sex the same and compares a cooler month with a warmer month. Female wing centroid size being smaller in September than in July connects cooler collection conditions with lower body-size proxy values. The answer is D.
